_meta.author: Claude (national autopilot batch)
_meta.note: VA federal House batch: Vindman (D, VA-07 incumbent), Beyer (D, VA-08 incumbent), Vega (R, sitting PWC supervisor — NOT running federal 2026, no FEC filing), Gray (R, VA-07 — WITHDREW from primary), Washington (R, VA-07 — running, primary Jun 16). Evidence from congress.gov roll calls, Heritage Action, Giffords / PPAF / NARAL endorsements, candidate-site Issues pages, Ballotpedia, FEC, local press. Several cross-party deviations flagged in notes (Vindman YES on parental-consent-gender H.R.2616 — 1 of 8 Ds; Vindman YES on Iran War Powers Article I assertion; Vindman cosponsor LOCAL Foods Act + NO on Farm Bill citing Bayer/Monsanto liability shield; Beyer Israel-aid NAY 2024 + War Powers stance). Cells with no individually-cited evidence left null.

profile.background: U.S. Representative VA-07 since Jan 3, 2025. Retired U.S. Army Colonel / JAG officer (25 yrs). Twin brother of Lt. Col. Alexander Vindman (Trump first-impeachment witness). Born in Soviet Union (Ukraine), naturalized as a child.

eugene-vindman.notes_set: U.S. Rep, VA-07 (D), since Jan 2025. [2026-06-02 national-autopilot refinement, federal tier] Solidly progressive on most issues with several documented conservative-aligned deviations. HARD: Heritage Action 0% lifetime; cosponsor of WHPA (H.R.12, 119th); Planned Parenthood Action Fund 2026 endorsement; Giffords + Everytown endorsed (supports universal background checks, national red-flag, AWB-era restrictions); NO on Laken Riley Act (first vote as Member); NO on SAVE Act (H.R.22); joined call to impeach DHS Sec Noem; opposed Stafford ICE detention center; Ukraine/Russia hawk. DEVIATION (parental rights / family sovereignty): voted YES on H.R.2616 'Stopping Indoctrination and Protecting Kids Act' (parental consent before schools change a student's pronouns / sex-based accommodations) — one of only 8 Democrats; his press release: 'Vindman Votes to Keep Parents Involved.' DEVIATION (foreign-policy restraint): voted YES on Iran War Powers Resolution (Roll Call 85, 119th) explicitly citing Article I Section 8. DEVIATION (industry capture / small-farm): cosponsor of LOCAL Foods Act with Rep. Baird (R-IN) (direct-to-consumer livestock sales, USDA inspection exemption); voted NO on Farm Bill citing pesticide-manufacturer liability shield (Bayer/Monsanto). Sitting member; running for reelection 2026 (Aug 4 primary). Note: he briefly announced (Feb 2026) intent to run in a redrawn VA-01 under a mid-decade map, but the VA Supreme Court struck down the referendum (May 2026), so the race reverts to VA-07.

0.note: Campaign issues page pledges to 'fight to stop any attempt to ban reproductive healthcare' and to 'restore the protections of Roe nationwide by passing the Women's Health Protection Act' — rejects personhood/conception framework.

1.note: Cosponsor of the Women's Health Protection Act (H.R.12, 119th) — the federal abortion-rights codification; opposite of abolition.

2.note: Campaign issues page explicitly defends IVF access.

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Action Fund 2026 (HARD evidence of abortion-industry alignment).

0.note: Confirmed member of the Congressional Equality Caucus (LGBTQ-rights bloc).

1.note: Equality Caucus member; supports state codification of same-sex marriage.

2.note: Voted NO on H.R.28 Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act (would bar biological males from girls' sports); not among the D crossovers.

4.note: Equality Caucus member; supports promotion of LGBTQ+ identity in policy.

1.note: DEVIATION: voted YES on H.R.2616 (May 2026) requiring parental consent before schools change a student's pronouns, preferred name, or sex-based accommodations — one of only 8 Democrats. His statement: 'parents should remain at the center of decisions involving their children at school.'
Voted NO on the SAVE Act (H.R.22, Roll Call 102, Apr 10, 2025) — would require documentary proof of citizenship to register to vote.
Voted NO on Laken Riley Act (H.R.29) — first vote as a Member — opposing detention of illegal aliens charged with theft/violent crimes.

2.note: Opposed Stafford ICE detention center as 'cruel to the core'; joined 110+ Ds demanding ICE accountability + Sec Noem impeachment — opposite of federal preemption against sanctuary policies.

0.note: Giffords-endorsed: supports universal background checks, closing gun-show loophole, training/certification/licensing — opposes constitutional carry framework.

1.note: Giffords + Everytown endorsed: supports national red-flag laws, safe-storage, training/licensing mandates.

0.note: DEVIATION: voted YES on Iran War Powers Resolution (Roll Call 85, 119th) explicitly invoking Article I §8 ('Article 1 Section 8 explicitly requires a vote by Congress to declare war').

2.note: DEVIATION: voted NO on 2026 Farm Bill citing 'shielding pesticide manufacturers from liability' (i.e. Bayer/Monsanto liability shield) — anti-Big-Ag-capture framing.

3.note: DEVIATION: cosponsor of the LOCAL Foods Act with Rep. Baird (R-IN) — exempts small-farm direct-to-consumer livestock sales from USDA inspection requirements.

profile.background: U.S. Representative VA-08 since 2015 (Arlington / Alexandria). Former VA Lt. Governor 1990-1998. Former U.S. Ambassador to Switzerland & Liechtenstein. Chair, Joint Economic Committee (Democratic side). Announced 2026 reelection bid June 27, 2025.

don-beyer.notes_set: U.S. Rep, VA-08 (D), since 2015 — deep voting record. [2026-06-02 national-autopilot refinement, federal tier] Long, consistent progressive record at the floor of the RESOLUTE rubric. HARD: Heritage Action 9% session / 6% lifetime; cosponsor of the Equality Act and Respect for Marriage Act (YES votes); WHPA cosponsor; NAY on H.R.7 No Taxpayer Funding for Abortion; NAY on Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act (H.R.21, 2025 & H.R.26, 2023); PPAF + NARAL endorsed; author of the 'Assault Weapons Excise Act' (1,000% excise tax on AR-platform rifles + high-capacity magazines); cosponsor & YES on the assault-weapons-ban package; Giffords endorsed; H.R.1 For the People Act YES; NAY on Laken Riley Act (Jan 2025); NAY on Parents Bill of Rights Act (party-line, March 2023). E0 DEVIATION (against the rubric): as JEC Chair, AUTHORED the Digital Asset Market Structure and Investor Protection Act that would empower the Federal Reserve to issue its own digital dollar — affirmatively pro-CBDC. I0 DEVIATION (toward the rubric): publicly defended Congress's Article I war powers (April 2026, Iran resolution; Jan 2020 Iran WPR signatory). I2 / J-Israel observation: voted NO on the April 2024 Israel military-aid tranche while supporting Ukraine aid — atypical for a senior House D, motivated by U.S.-assistance-law concerns rather than rubric framing.

0.note: Public stance: 'I strongly support a woman's right to choose.' Rejects personhood-from-conception framework.

1.note: WHPA cosponsor + YES on House passage; NAY on H.R.7 No Taxpayer Funding for Abortion.

3.note: NAY on Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act (H.R.26, Jan 2023, RC 29; H.R.21, Jan 2025, RC 27); SBA characterizes him as voting to 'eliminate or prevent protections' for born-alive infants.

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Action Fund and NARAL; pledged to 'fight attempts to cut funding for Planned Parenthood.'

0.note: Voted YES on Respect for Marriage Act (H.R.8404, July 19 2022 and Dec 8 2022 concurrence); celebratory press statement.

1.note: RFMA YES — federal codification of same-sex marriage recognition.

2.note: Cosponsor of the Equality Act (H.R.5, 117th) — extends civil-rights protections by gender identity, opposing the immutable-biological-sex framework.

4.note: Equality Act cosponsor; House Issues page lists LGBTQ rights as a priority.

1.note: NAY on Parents Bill of Rights Act (H.R.5, 118th, March 24, 2023) — all D caucus voted NAY on this party-line roll call requiring parental notification on curriculum + gender-related school services.
Equality Act cosponsor — bill explicitly narrows RFRA conscience defenses for religious medical providers, adoption agencies, and businesses.

0.note: As JEC Chair, AUTHORED the Digital Asset Market Structure and Investor Protection Act, which would 'empower the Federal Reserve to issue its own digital dollar' — affirmatively pro-CBDC.

1.note: Voted YES on H.R.1 For the People Act (March 3, 2021) — bill explicitly preempts state photo voter ID requirements.

2.note: H.R.1 YES — bill mandates expanded early voting and limits single-day in-person regimes.

3.note: H.R.1 YES — bill mandates universal no-excuse mail-in voting and drop-box expansion.

1.note: NAY on Laken Riley Act (S.5, Jan 22 2025, RC 23) — opposed detention of illegal aliens charged with theft/violent crime.

0.note: Giffords-endorsed; YES on Bipartisan Background Checks, Bipartisan Safer Communities Act — opposes constitutional carry.

1.note: Author of the Assault Weapons Excise Act (1,000% excise tax on AR-platform rifles + high-capacity magazines); YES on House AWB passage July 29, 2022.

0.note: DEVIATION: defended Congress's Article I war powers; joined April 2026 D effort to force a vote on Iran war-powers resolution; signed onto Jan 2020 Iran WPR.

set.office: U.S. Representative VA-07 (NOT running 2026 — sitting Prince William Co. Supervisor / 2022 + 2024 R nominee)

profile.background: Sitting Prince William County Supervisor, Coles District (R) — second term. Career law-enforcement / sheriff's-deputy background; Salvadoran heritage. Two-time R nominee for VA-07 federal House: 2022 (lost to Spanberger 47.6%) and 2024 (per existing roster notes; the 2024 nominee per other sources was Derrick Anderson — see notes). NOT filed with FEC for 2026 federal cycle as of 2026-06-02; campaign site currently frames her as running for Coles re-election, not Congress.

yesli-vega.notes_set: Prince William Co. Supervisor (Coles District, R) — sitting. CANDIDACY-CURRENCY FIX (2026-06-02): NOT running for federal VA-07 in 2026 — no FEC F2 filing on record for 2025-2026 cycle; FEC profile H2VA07261 last shows 2022 cycle. Existing roster note flagging a 'may run 2026' federal candidacy is stale. Set candidacy_status: not_running. The active R-primary field for VA-07 (Aug 4, 2026) is Durant, Gray (withdrew), Mayfield, Ollivant, Smithers, Washington — Vega not among them. [2026-06-02 national-autopilot refinement, federal tier — scoring her 2022/2024 federal-nominee record + sitting-supervisor positions] HARD: SBA Pro-Life America endorsed 2022; FRC Action PAC endorsed 2022; Trump 'Complete and Total' endorsement Oct 2022; Ginni Thomas primary endorsement. Pro-life: 'only God – not a doctor – can decide the life of the mother.' Marriage: 'I support the traditional definition of marriage.' Anti-trans-ideology: tanning-bed analogy + anti-mutilation statements; voted AGAINST PWC Pride Month proclamations 2020/21/22 and against PWC code compliance with the Virginia Values Act. Anti-CRT publicly. Pro-school-choice (Noah Webster Educational Foundation endorsed). Pro-border-barrier + pro-ICE-cooperation (defended ICE in PWC). Anti-gun-control supervisor record. Anti-vaccine-mandate: personally attended PWC School Board meeting Feb 2022 to support reversal of staff vaccine-or-test mandate. F-category (Election integrity): hedged on 2020 — said 'no' when pressed on stolen, so partial-credit cells left null. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on E (economic stewardship details), I (foreign policy), or D (Christian liberty self-statements).

0.note: Vega quote: 'only God – not a doctor – can decide the life of the mother' — affirms conception-personhood framework. SBA Pro-Life America endorsed 2022.

4.note: Planned Parenthood denounced her as an 'anti-reproductive health extremist' (HARD signal of no PP funding relationship); no record of NARAL or EMILY's List money.

0.note: Vega: 'I support the traditional definition of marriage' (1m-1w). Aligned with Sen. Cruz on overturning Obergefell.

1.note: Aligned with Cruz position favoring overturning the constitutional same-sex-marriage decision.

2.note: Quote: 'You have to be 18 years old to get under a tanning bed, but the Democratic Party… [is] saying it's OK for children to literally mutilate their bodies. That's unacceptable.' Affirms biological-sex framework.

4.note: Voted AGAINST PWC Pride Month proclamations 2020/2021/2022 and against PWC code compliance with the Virginia Values Act. Quote: 'schools don't exist for left-wing activists to groom students towards a sexual preference.'

0.note: Endorsed/interviewed by Noah Webster Educational Foundation (school-choice org); signaled parental-control-of-education stance.

1.note: Public support for parental notification/consent on gender-related school services: 'It protects children… it gives parents in the know as to what is happening with their children.'

2.note: Publicly criticized CRT — said proponents 'view unequal outcomes as proof of discrimination.'

0.note: Vowed to 'secure U.S. border' at 2022 Spotsylvania office opening; campaigned on completed border barrier.

2.note: As PWC Supervisor publicly defended ICE cooperation: 'an issue about allowing these predators to continue to victimize members of our immigrant communities.' Opposed sanctuary policies.

1.note: Vega: 'As Prince William County Supervisor, I fought against gun control resolutions that encouraged the Virginia General Assembly to take away this right.' Opposes red-flag / mag-limit / AWB framework.

0.note: Personally attended PWC School Board meeting Feb 2022 to support reversal of staff vaccine-or-test mandate — pro-informed-consent posture.

set.office: U.S. Representative VA-07 (WITHDREW 2026 R primary · former 2023 VA HD-25 nominee · Vietnam-era USMC vet, CPA)

profile.background: WITHDREW from the 2026 VA-07 Republican primary before the May 26, 2026 filing deadline. Vietnam-era U.S. Marine veteran (4-yr enlistee, Sergeant, honorable discharge); CPA in Prince William County. Founder/Treasurer of three FQHCs in PWC. Prior 2023 R nominee for Virginia House of Delegates HD-25 (lost to Briana Sewell). FEC committee 'John Gray for Virginia' (C00902775); Statement of Candidacy Apr 18, 2025; raised ~$229k before withdrawing; 2026 campaign website now a Squarespace 'Private Site' lockout page.

john-gray-va-07.notes_set: U.S. Representative VA-07 candidate (R) — WITHDREW. CANDIDACY-CURRENCY FIX (2026-06-02): Ballotpedia lists his 2026 status as 'Withdrew primary'; the VA-07 election page lists him under 'Withdrawn or disqualified candidates' for the Aug 4, 2026 R primary. 'He will not appear on the ballot for the Republican primary on August 4, 2026' (Ballotpedia bio, verbatim). FEC filing was opened (raised $228,951, spent $122,914) before withdrawal. Campaign site (johngrayforvirginia.com) is now a Squarespace 'Private Site' lockout. Likely trigger: April 21, 2026 Virginia Supreme Court strike-down of the redistricting referendum + consolidation of the R field. Set candidacy_status: not_running. [2026-06-02 refinement, federal tier — scoring the thin 2023 VA-25 House-of-Delegates campaign-site statements that remain publicly verifiable] HARD: 'Boys don't belong in girls sports'; 'support school choice'; 'defend parental rights'; 'fight to protect minor children and pass Sage's Law' (VA parental-notification-on-gender bill); 'Public schools should educate and not indoctrinate.' Did NOT complete Ballotpedia Candidate Connection in either 2023 or 2026. No documented positions on sanctity of life, marriage definition, Christian liberty self-statement, economic stewardship specifics (CBDC / sound money / BBA), election integrity, border, 2A, foreign policy, or industry capture. Disambiguation OK: this is John Gray, CPA, PWC, Vietnam-era Marine vet — distinct from the 19th-c. Rep. John Gray of VA-20.

2.note: 2023 VA-25 House-of-Delegates campaign site (still live as the only verifiable issues page): 'Boys don't belong in girls sports' — affirms biological-sex framework in athletics.

1.note: Campaign site: 'fight to protect minor children and pass Sage's Law' (Virginia parental-notification-on-gender-identity bill) + 'defend parental rights.' Specific bill endorsement — HARD.

set.office: U.S. Representative VA-07 (2026 R primary candidate · Aug 4 primary · West Point grad, combat vet, former Tuberville Defense Fellow)

profile.background: U.S. Military Academy at West Point (Black Knights football roster 2010-2012; Fork Union Military Academy prior); commissioned U.S. Army officer; combat veteran. Defense Fellow for Sen. Tommy Tuberville (R-AL), Jan-Dec 2024 (LegiStorm). 2026 VA-07 R primary candidate on the August 4 ballot.

waverly-washington.notes_set: U.S. Representative VA-07 candidate (R) — primary candidate (Aug 4, 2026 R primary). [2026-06-02 national-autopilot refinement] CANDIDACY-CURRENCY: confirmed candidate via Ballotpedia + InsideNoVa; campaign site live at waveforcongress.com; R primary field is Durant / Mayfield / Ollivant / Washington / others. (Politics1's VA roster omits him but Ballotpedia + InsideNoVa confirm — Politics1 stale.) Online policy footprint is thin: campaign site's only scoreable content is generic 'cutting taxes' and opposition to 'out-of-control government spending' (insufficient to score the BBA cell specifically). NO Ballotpedia Candidate Connection completed. NO findable issues page (paths return 404); X handle (@wavecongress) returns 402. NO documented positions on sanctity of life, marriage, family/child sovereignty, Christian liberty, CBDC, election integrity, border, 2A, foreign policy, or industry capture. NO endorsement-org listings located (SBA Pro-Life, NRA-PVF, FRC, Family Foundation of VA, VCDL all silent on him in this sweep). The 'Army Reservist' descriptor in the existing roster note is plausible (West Point grad → IRR/Reserve post-service) but not directly verified in open sources. Recommended re-poll June 17 once the R primary result is in — if he loses, flip candidacy_status to 'lost' and status to 'lost'. Confidence: roster_verified (verified he holds candidacy + military background, but no documented policy positions to score federally).
